what_it_does: A JSON interpretive-ledger entry for the claude-ds source file app/ai/ai-capabilities-canvas.js; it records what the source IIFE does, lists observations, standouts, conventions, concerns, questions, themes, inferred intent, novelty, contribution, and embedding summaries, and declares no code symbols.
observations: The file is a structured interpretive layer keyed by project, path, and model ('opus').
It documents an IIFE that registers per-canvas generative moves into window.CV_AI as first-class capabilities.
It enumerates ~28 text capabilities across color/icon/voice/pattern/component/build/deck/widget/wizard/type/inbox/chat families and 2 image capabilities.
It notes a single bespoke capability, 'deck.titlechain', with an inlined prompt implementing DESIGN-LANGUAGE §16.
It records load-order enforcement: the source IIFE throws if window.CV_AI is missing and suggests loading ai-registry.js first.
It describes a generic-run convention where runText/runImgGen/runImgEdit complete canvas-supplied prompts rather than duplicating them.
It includes a 'purpose_vs_actual' field that says the source's stated purpose matches its behavior, with deck.titlechain as a noted exception.
It ends with empty symbols[] and embedding-friendly summary/intention sentences.
standouts: Catalogue-as-dispatch: the data arrays of capability tuples are treated as both the generative surface description and the registry inspector projection.
Only one capability, deck.titlechain, breaks the generic-run/prompt-single-sourced convention by inlining its rule-prompt.
The interpretive record mixes factual description, pattern conventions, inferred intention, and a localized concern about prompt duplication.
conventions: Follows the interpretive-layer schema with neutral, file-local commentary and no ranking.
Uses list fields (observations, standouts, conventions, concerns, questions, apparent_themes) to keep notes atomic.
Includes embedding-oriented summary and intention sentences for downstream retrieval.
Declares an empty symbols array because the described source file has no extractable code symbols in this layer.
concerns: The record contains inferred intent and novelty judgments without showing the source lines that support them.
It notes a source-level inconsistency (deck.titlechain inlining a prompt) but does not quantify its impact.
No line numbers, excerpts, or confidence markers are provided, making it harder to verify claims against the source.
notes: This is a second-order interpretive JSON that summarizes the behavior, patterns, and concerns of the ai-capabilities-canvas.js source file for ledger/fusion consumption.
questions: What schema version governs this interpretive layer?
How were the ~30 capability ids and families enumerated — by static scan or manual curation?
Does a downstream consumer treat the embedding summary fields as the canonical description or as supplementary?
What is the relationship between this interpretive file and the actual source file it describes?
purpose_vs_actual: Match — the file presents itself as an interpretive ledger entry for a source file and delivers structured description, observations, conventions, concerns, themes, and summaries consistent with that purpose.
intention_signals: To produce a machine-readable, queryable interpretation of a source file so that a fused ledger can understand its generative surface, conventions, and localized inconsistencies without reading the source directly.
novelty: It is a meta-document that treats a code file as a design substrate and records not only factual behavior but inferred themes, contribution, and embedding-friendly summaries.
contribution: Contributes a structured, file-local interpretation of ai-capabilities-canvas.js, including its capability-tuple shape, generic-run convention, deck.titlechain exception, load-order dependency, and embedding summaries, useful for cross-file queries about the AI capability registry surface.
summary_for_embedding: JSON interpretive-ledger entry describing an IIFE that registers ~30 per-canvas Vi generative capabilities into CV_AI as data tuples with generic prompt-completing runs, noting a single inlined-prompt exception and load-order enforcement.
intention_for_embedding: To provide a neutral, file-local, machine-readable summary of the ai-capabilities-canvas.js source file for downstream ledger fusion and retrieval.
